content: Shipped the local-first LLM adapter pilot: new lib/llm/ module (LlmClient interface, LocalLlmClient for oMLX/Qwen, AnthropicLlmClient, RouterLlmClient) wired into think curate. Default-local with Anthropic fallback on size overflow.
context: Pilot only wires the curate path; other 7 call sites unchanged.
decisions: ["Default to local LLM, fall back to Anthropic only on size overflow or explicit opt-in", "Local calls bypass the consent gate since data stays on device"]

content: Decided to keep the Claude Agent SDK as the Anthropic backend rather than switching to the raw Messages API, to preserve subscription billing and keep the pilot a zero-behavior-change diff until a user configures a local endpoint.
decisions: ["Keep Agent SDK for the Anthropic path in the local-first pilot"]

content: Fast-forwarded think-cli main 603 commits to match github/main; repo restructured into packages/cli workspace with CI publish/test workflows and a Dockerfile.

Investigated hal9k's local LLM adapter as the model for think's: env-var-selected OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions to oMLX/Qwen, no automatic fallback, special-token stripping. think will add the fallback policy hal9k lacks.
debug: console.log left in router.ts, removed before commit

Added 34 unit tests for the LLM adapter using injected fake clients (no network, no SDK): router policy table, LocalLlmClient overflow/token-stripping, runCuration with fakes. Full suite 454 passing, typecheck clean.

Considering hosting the Qwen model on a shared server (Railway hivedb) so server-side think curation can route locally instead of to the Anthropic API. Open question: MLX builds are Apple-Silicon only; Railway has no GPU.
